---
title: "Basic Data Visualisation, How to make tables in R: Exercise solution"
output: html_document
---

---
This block contains instructions on how to fill in this template.

**Please delete this block when creating your own file.**

This template should be used to create code snippet notebooks for each chapter
in a learning resource. Follow the instructions below to complete the template:
- Replace content in square brackets (e.g., [unit_name], [link], [chapter name])
with the relevant information. For example the title block below could be changed
from "[unit_name], [chapter_name] - Code snippets" to "Basic Data Visualisation,
Creating Bar Charts - Code snippets".
- In the setup section add any instructions for users to follow before running the
code snippets, such as installing packages or downloading data files. Add any
necessary import statements or setup code in the first code cell.
- Code snippets should be grouped into sections according to the sections in the
learning resource.
- Each code snippet from the learning resource should be placed in its own code
cell under the relevant section, with any necessary context provided in a
markdown block above each code cell.
- Follow [tidyverse](https://style.tidyverse.org/) style guide for R code
formatting.
- Whenever you need to reference a file in [learning_resources](http://_vscodecontentref_/1), use:
```r
file_path <- file.path(learning_resources_dir, "data", "filename.csv")
```

- Definitions:
    - "Unit name" refers to the unit name, which is also the name of the folder
    this python folder is in.
    - "Chapter name" refers to the specific chapter that the code snippets are
    in. Chapters are the highest level headings in the learning resource.
    - "Section name" refers to subheadings within chapters that code snippets
    are in.
---

# Basic Data Visualisation, How to make tables in R: Exercise solution

The following code relates to the **Making tables in R** script in the
**Basic Data Visualisation** learning resource on the [Analysis for Action]([PLACEHOLDER])
platform.

This is the code used to produce the exercise solution from the **Making tables in R** script. Compare your code and output against the code and output produced here to see how well you did. Note that your code and output do not need to match the solution exactly to be considered correct!

Can you think of any other ways of making the table more accessible?

The output should:

* Show the size of vulnerable populations in Argentina, Malawi, Nepal, and the United Kingdom for the years 1982, 1987, and 1992
* Include a summary column showing the mean average size of vulnerable populations across these three years
* Include a spanner heading above the 1982, 1987, and 1992 columns called ‘Vulnerable population by year’
* Include a stubhead label heading above the countries column called 'Developer'
*	Have commas to separate thousands
* Be formatted so that:
  * The spanner heading is a dark green with a HEX code of #324027 and the text is white
  * The stubhead label heading is a dark green with a HEX code of #324027 and the text is white and bold
  * The column labels are an earthy green with a HEX code of #48553F and the text is white

## Troubleshooting

If you encounter any issues running the code snippets, consider the following
troubleshooting steps:
- Ensure you have followed the setup instructions in the
[README.md](../../../README.md) file including `install.packages()` for
required packages.
- Run code from top to bottom to ensure all dependencies are met.
- Restart your R session and clear all outputs.
- For more detailed troubleshooting guidance, refer to the
[troubleshooting document]([PLACEHOLDER]).

## Setup

Before running the code snippets, ensure you have:
- followed the setup instructions in the [README.md](../../../README.md) file,
- **[add further instructions as needed]**,
- run the code block below.

```{r setup, message=FALSE}
# This chunk should include:
# * library() calls for required packages
# * paths to data files
# * any other setup required

library(readr)
library(here)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(janitor)
library(gt)

# Use here::here() to construct paths, do not setwd()
here::here()
vuln <- read.csv(here::here("learning_resources", "data", "vulnerable.csv"))
```

## Prepare the dataframe

```{r}

# Prepare and shape the data to include a summary column

vuln_exercise_df <- vuln %>%
  
  filter(year %in% c("1982", "1987", "1992")) %>%
  
  filter(country %in% c("Argentina", "Malawi", "Nepal", "United Kingdom")) %>% # Selecting the countries in this order saves me having to re-order them later
  
  select(country, year, vulnerable_pop) %>%
  
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = year, values_from = vulnerable_pop) %>%
  
  tidyr::drop_na()

vuln_exercise_df <- janitor::clean_names(vuln_exercise_df)

vuln_exercise_df <- vuln_exercise_df %>%
  
  rename_with(~ sub("^x", "", .x))

# Add a column for mean average
# Make sure columns are numeric first (otherwise it won't be possible to calculate an average)

vuln_exercise_df <- vuln_exercise_df %>%
  
  mutate(
    
    `1982` = as.numeric(gsub(",", "", `1982`)),
    
    `1987` = as.numeric(gsub(",", "", `1987`)),
    
    `1992` = as.numeric(gsub(",", "", `1992`))
    
  ) %>%
  
  mutate(Average = rowMeans(across(c(`1982`, `1987`, `1992`)), na.rm = TRUE))


```

## Create and format the table as per requirements

```{r}

# Make the base table

vuln_exercise <- vuln_exercise_df %>%
  
  gt(rowname_col = "country") %>%
  
  # Add a spanner heading 
  
  tab_spanner(label = gt::md("**Vulnerable population by year**"), columns = everything()) %>%
  
  # Add a stubhead label above the countries
  
  tab_stubhead(label = "Developers") %>%

  # Format numbers to include commas

  fmt_number(columns = everything(),

             use_seps = TRUE,

             decimals = 0) %>%

  # Change the colour of the spanner / header and make the text white (spanners are already bold by default)

  tab_style(
    
              style = list(
                
                cell_fill(color = "#324027"),
                
                cell_text(color = "white")
                
              ),
              
              locations = cells_column_spanners(everything())
                
              ) %>%
  
  # Change the colour of the stubhead label and make the text bold and white

  tab_style(
    
              style = list(
                
                cell_fill(color = "#324027"),
                
                cell_text(weight = "bold", color = "white")
                
              ),
              
              locations = cells_stubhead()
                
              ) %>%

  # Change the colour of the column labels and make the text white

  tab_style(
    
              style = list(
                                
                cell_fill(color = "#48553F"),
                
                cell_text(color = 'white')
                
              ),
              
              locations = cells_column_labels()
                
              )

vuln_exercise # Display the output

```

## Export the table

```{r}

# Save the table as HTML

vuln_exercise %>%

  gtsave(filename = "r_table_solution.html", path = "D:/TEMP/")
```
